sections.name: Potentially Adverse Event Analysis
sections.description: Potentially adverse events are analyzed to better understand associated activities

sections.name: Event Information Correlation
sections.description: Information is correlated from multiple sources

sections.name: Impact & Scope Determination
sections.description: The estimated impact and scope of adverse events are understood

sections.name: Event Information Sharing
sections.description: Information on adverse events is provided to authorized staff and tools

sections.name: Contextual Analysis
sections.description: Cyber threat intelligence and other contextual information are integrated into the analysis

sections.name: Incident Declaration
sections.description: Incidents are declared when adverse events meet the defined incident criteria

sections.name: Network Monitoring
sections.description: Networks and network services are monitored to find potentially adverse events

sections.name: Physical Environment Monitoring
sections.description: The physical environment is monitored to find potentially adverse events

sections.name: Personnel Activity Monitoring
sections.description: Personnel activity and technology usage are monitored to find potentially adverse events

sections.name: Service Provider Monitoring
sections.description: External service provider activities and services are monitored to find potentially adverse events

sections.name: Hardware, Software, & Data Monitoring
sections.description: Computing hardware and software, runtime environments, and their data are monitored to find potentially adverse events

sections.name: Contract General Requirements
sections.description: Contracts clearly specify the rights and responsibilities of each party and establish requirements to address the anticipated risks posed by a third party over the life of the relationship

sections.name: Contract Cybersecurity-Related Requirements
sections.description: Expected cybersecurity practices for critical third parties that meet the risk management objectives of the organization are identified, documented, and agreed

principles.name: EXTEND: Procurement Planning and Due Diligence

sections.name: Procurement Planning
sections.description: Planning is performed for procurements and agreements that involve elevated risk to the organization

sections.name: Prospective Third Party Due Diligence
sections.description: The organization performs thorough due diligence on prospective third parties, consistent with procurement planning and commensurate with the level of risk, criticality, and complexity of each third-party relationship

sections.name: Technology & Cybersecurity Risk Assessment
sections.description: The organization assesses the risks and suitability of the technology and cybersecurity capabilities and risk management practices of prospective third parties

sections.name: Product & Service Due Diligence
sections.description: Third-party products and services are assessed relative to business, risk management, and cybersecurity requirements

sections.name: Ongoing Third-Party Monitoring
sections.description: Critical suppliers and third parties are monitored to confirm that they continue to satisfy their obligations as required; reviews of audits, test results, or other assessments of third parties are conducted

sections.name: Ongoing Third-Party Management
sections.description: Inter-dependent and coordinated cybersecurity risk management practices with third parties are managed to ensure ongoing effectiveness

sections.name: Termination Planning
sections.description: The organization anticipates and plans for the termination of critical relationships under both normal and adverse circumstances

sections.name: Termination Practices
sections.description: Relationship terminations and the return or destruction of assets are performed in a controlled and safe manner

sections.name: Independent Audit Compliance Assessment
sections.description: The independent audit function assesses compliance with internal controls and applicable laws and regulations

sections.name: Independent Audit Procedures
sections.description: The independent audit function updates its procedures and audit plans to adjust to the evolving technology and cybersecurity environment

sections.name: Independent Audit Reporting
sections.description: The independent audit function identifies, tracks, and reports significant changes in the organization's risk exposure to the governing authority (e.g., the Board or one of its committees)

sections.name: Independent Risk Management Function
sections.description: The independent risk management function provides assurance that the technology and cybersecurity risk management frameworks have been implemented according to policy and are consistent with the organization's risk appetite and tolerance

sections.name: Independent Risk Management Evaluation
sections.description: The independent risk management function evaluates the appropriateness of the risk management program for the organization's risk appetite and proposes program improvements as warranted

sections.name: Independent Risk Management Reporting
sections.description: The independent risk management function reports on the implementation of the technology and cybersecurity risk management frameworks to the governing authority (e.g., the Board or one of its committees)

sections.name: Organizational Mission
sections.description: The organizational mission is understood and informs technology and cybersecurity risk management

sections.name: Stakeholder Risk Management Expectations
sections.description: Internal and external stakeholders are understood, and their needs and expectations regarding technology and cybersecurity risk management are understood and considered

sections.name: Legal, Regulatory, & Contractual Requirements
sections.description: Legal, regulatory, and contractual requirements regarding technology and cybersecurity - including privacy and civil liberties obligations - are understood and managed

sections.name: Stakeholder Service Expectations
sections.description: Critical objectives, capabilities, and services that stakeholders depend on or expect from the organization are understood and communicated

sections.name: Organizational Service Dependencies
sections.description: Outcomes, capabilities, and services that the organization depends on are understood and communicated

sections.name: Risk Management Strategy Outcomes Review
sections.description: Technology and cybersecurity risk management strategy outcomes are reviewed to inform and adjust strategy and direction

sections.name: Risk Management Strategy Review & Adjustment
sections.description: The technology and cybersecurity risk management strategies are reviewed and adjusted to ensure coverage of organizational requirements and risks

sections.name: Risk Management Performance Measurement
sections.description: Organizational technology and cybersecurity risk management performance is evaluated and reviewed for adjustments needed

sections.name: Establishment of Policies & Procedures
sections.description: Policies for managing technology and cybersecurity risks are established based on organizational context, cybersecurity strategy, and priorities and is communicated and enforced

sections.name: Policy & Procedure Review & Update
sections.description: Policies for managing technology and cybersecurity risks are reviewed, updated, communicated, and enforced to reflect changes in requirements, threats, technology, and organizational mission

sections.name: Risk Management Objectives Agreement
sections.description: Risk management objectives are established and agreed to by organizational stakeholders

sections.name: Risk Appetite & Risk Tolerance Statements
sections.description: Risk appetite and risk tolerance statements are established, communicated, and maintained

sections.name: Enterprise Risk Integration
sections.description: Technology and cybersecurity risk management activities and outcomes are included in enterprise risk management processes

sections.name: Risk Response Strategic Direction
sections.description: Strategic direction that describes appropriate risk response options is established and communicated

sections.name: Lines of Communication
sections.description: Lines of communication across the organization are established for technology and cybersecurity risks, including risks from suppliers and other third parties

sections.name: Standardized Risk Management Method
sections.description: A standardized method for calculating, documenting, categorizing, and prioritizing technology and cybersecurity risks is established and communicated

sections.name: Strategic Opportunities
sections.description: Strategic opportunities (i.e., positive risks) are characterized and are included in organizational technology and cybersecurity risk discussions

sections.name: Technology Assimilation & Implementations
sections.description: The risks of technology assimilation and implementations are managed

sections.name: Business Continuity & Resilience Risk Management
sections.description: The organization's business continuity and resilience requirement risks are managed

sections.name: Organizational Leadership Responsibility
sections.description: Organizational leadership is responsible and accountable for technology and cybersecurity risks and fosters a culture that is risk-aware, ethical, and continually improving

sections.name: Risk Management Roles & Responsibilities
sections.description: Roles, responsibilities, and authorities related to technology and cybersecurity risk management are established, communicated, understood, and enforced

sections.name: Resource Adequacy
sections.description: Adequate resources are allocated commensurate with technology and cybersecurity risk strategy, roles, responsibilities, and policies

sections.name: Human Resource Practices
sections.description: Cybersecurity is included in human resources practices

principles.name: GOVERN: Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Management

sections.name: Supply Chain Risk Management Program
sections.description: A supply chain risk management program, strategy, objectives, policies, and processes are established and agreed to by organizational stakeholders

sections.name: Third Party Roles & Responsibilities
sections.description: Roles and responsibilities for suppliers, customers, and partners are established, communicated, and coordinated internally and externally

sections.name: Supply Chain Risk Management Integration
sections.description: Supply chain risk management is integrated into cybersecurity and enterprise risk management, risk assessment, and improvement processes

sections.name: Supplier Identification & Prioritization
sections.description: Suppliers are known and prioritized by criticality

sections.name: Third Party Incident Management Integration
sections.description: Relevant suppliers and other third parties are included in incident planning, response, and recovery activities

sections.name: Supply Chain Security Practice Integration
sections.description: Supply chain security practices are integrated into technology, cybersecurity, and enterprise risk management programs, and their performance is monitored throughout the technology product and service life cycle

sections.name: Hardware Inventory
sections.description: Inventories of hardware managed by the organization are maintained

sections.name: Software, Services, & Systems Inventory
sections.description: Inventories of software, services, and systems managed by the organization are maintained

sections.name: Network Communications & Data Flows
sections.description: Representations of the organization’s authorized network communication and internal and external network data flows are maintained

sections.name: Supplier Services Inventory
sections.description: Inventories of services provided by suppliers are maintained

sections.name: Asset Protection Prioritization
sections.description: Assets are prioritized based on classification, criticality, resources, and impact on the mission

sections.name: Data & Metadata Inventory
sections.description: Inventories of data and corresponding metadata for designated data types are maintained

sections.name: Asset Life Cycle Management
sections.description: Systems, hardware, software, services, and data are managed throughout their life cycles

sections.name: Continuous Improvements Evaluation
sections.description: Improvements are identified from evaluations

sections.name: Tests & Exercises
sections.description: Improvements are identified from tests and exercises, including those done in coordination with suppliers and relevant third parties

sections.name: Improvements from Lessons Learned
sections.description: Improvements are identified from execution of operational processes, procedures, and activities

sections.name: Plans Affecting Operations
sections.description: ID.IM-04: Response and recovery plans (e.g., incident response plan, business continuity plan, disaster recovery plan, etc.) are established, communicated, maintained, and improved

sections.name: Asset Vulnerability Identification
sections.description: Vulnerabilities in assets are identified, validated, and recorded

sections.name: Information Sharing Forums
sections.description: Cyber threat intelligence is received from information sharing forums and sources

sections.name: Threat Identification
sections.description: Internal and external threats to the organization are identified and recorded

sections.name: Impact & Likelihood Analysis
sections.description: Potential impacts and likelihoods of threats exploiting vulnerabilities are identified and recorded

sections.name: Risk Exposure Determination & Prioritization
sections.description: Threats, vulnerabilities, likelihoods, and impacts are used to understand inherent risk and inform risk response prioritization

sections.name: Risk Response Determination
sections.description: Risk responses are chosen, prioritized, planned, tracked, and communicated

sections.name: Change & Exception Management
sections.description: Changes and exceptions are managed, assessed for risk impact, recorded, and tracked

sections.name: Vulnerability Disclosure Response
sections.description: Processes for receiving, analyzing, and responding to vulnerability disclosures are established

principles.name: PROTECT: Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Control

sections.name: Identity & Credential Management
sections.description: Identities and credentials for authorized users, services, and hardware are managed by the organization

sections.name: Identity Binding to Credentials
sections.description: Identities are proofed and bound to credentials based on the context of interactions

sections.name: Authentication
sections.description: Users, services, and hardware are authenticated

sections.name: Identity Assertions
sections.description: Identity assertions are protected, conveyed, and verified

sections.name: Access Authorizations
sections.description: Access permissions, entitlements, and authorizations are defined in a policy, managed, enforced, and reviewed, and incorporate the principles of least privilege and separation of duties

sections.name: Physical Access
sections.description: Physical access to assets is managed, monitored, and enforced commensurate with risk

sections.name: User Awareness & Training
sections.description: Personnel are provided with awareness and training so that they possess the knowledge and skills to perform general tasks with cybersecurity risks in mind

sections.name: Specialized Role Awareness & Training
sections.description: Individuals in specialized roles are provided with awareness and training so that they possess the knowledge and skills to perform relevant tasks with cybersecurity risks in mind

sections.name: Protection of Data at Rest
sections.description: The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-at-rest are protected

sections.name: Protection of Data in Transit
sections.description: The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-in-transit are protected

sections.name: Protection of Data in Use
sections.description: The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-in-use are protected

sections.name: Data Backup
sections.description: Backups of data are created, protected, maintained, and tested

sections.name: Logical Access Protections
sections.description: Networks and environments are protected from unauthorized logical access and usage

sections.name: Environmental Threat Protections
sections.description: The organization’s technology assets are protected from environmental threats

sections.name: Resilience Measures
sections.description: Mechanisms are implemented to achieve resilience requirements in normal and adverse situations

sections.name: Capacity Management
sections.description: Adequate resource capacity to ensure availability is maintained

sections.name: Configuration Management
sections.description: Configuration management practices are established and applied

sections.name: Software Maintenance & Replacement
sections.description: Software is maintained, replaced, and removed commensurate with risk

sections.name: Hardware Maintenance
sections.description: Hardware is maintained, replaced, and removed commensurate with risk

sections.name: Log Record Generation
sections.description: Log records are generated and made available for continuous monitoring

sections.name: Unauthorized Software Installation & Execution
sections.description: Installation and execution of unauthorized software are prevented

sections.name: Secure Systems Development Practices
sections.description: Secure software development practices are integrated, and their performance is monitored throughout the software development life cycle

sections.name: Technology Operations, Service, & Support
sections.description: The organization's technology operations and service and support functions are designed and managed to address business operational needs and stakeholder requirements.

sections.name: Recovery Activity Communication
sections.description: Recovery activities and progress in restoring operational capabilities are communicated to designated internal and external stakeholders

sections.name: Public Information Sharing
sections.description: Public updates on incident recovery are shared using approved methods and messaging

sections.name: Recovery Plan Execution
sections.description: The recovery portion of the incident response plan is executed once initiated from the incident response process

sections.name: Recovery Action Performance
sections.description: Recovery actions are selected, scoped, prioritized, and performed

sections.name: Backup & Restoration Asset Integrity
sections.description: The integrity of backups and other restoration assets is verified before using them for restoration

sections.name: Post-Incident Operational Norms
sections.description: Critical mission functions and cybersecurity risk management are considered to establish post-incident operational norms

sections.name: Asset Integrity Restoration
sections.description: The integrity of restored assets is verified, systems and services are restored, and normal operating status is confirmed

sections.name: End-of-Incident Determination
sections.description: The end of incident recovery is declared based on criteria, and incident-related documentation is completed

sections.name: Incident Analysis & Root Cause Determination
sections.description: Analysis is performed to establish what has taken place during an incident and the root cause of the incident

sections.name: Investigation Documentation
sections.description: Actions performed during an investigation are recorded, and the records’ integrity and provenance are preserved

sections.name: Incident Data Collection & Preservation
sections.description: Incident data and metadata are collected, and their integrity and provenance are preserved

sections.name: Incident Magnitude Determination
sections.description: An incident's magnitude is estimated and validated

principles.name: RESPOND: Incident Response Reporting and Communication

sections.name: Stakeholder Incident Notification
sections.description: Internal and external stakeholders are notified of incidents

sections.name: Stakeholder Incident Information Sharing
sections.description: Information is shared with designated internal and external stakeholders

sections.name: Response Plan Execution
sections.description: The incident response plan is executed in coordination with relevant third parties once an incident is declared

sections.name: Incident Triage & Validation
sections.description: Incident reports are triaged and validated

sections.name: Incident Categorization & Prioritization
sections.description: Incidents are categorized and prioritized

sections.name: Incident Escalation
sections.description: Incidents are escalated or elevated as needed

sections.name: Recovery Initiation
sections.description: The criteria for initiating incident recovery are applied

sections.name: Incident Containment
sections.description: Incidents are contained

sections.name: Incident Eradication
sections.description: Incidents are eradicated
